page: Amazon Web Services m5n.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by bottom-quartile results in single-core stress-ng tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core capabilities are more robust, with average tier results in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is strong, placing in the top 25%, while small-block cached reads perform in the average tier.

This instance is configured for workloads that benefit from high network throughput and multi-threaded processing rather than single-core speed. Typical applications include static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks, which all demonstrate average tier performance in benchmarks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of local storage and average single-core performance are balanced by the high-density memory configuration and 75 Gbps network baseline, making it a viable option for network-bound general-purpose applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz with a 35 MB L3 cache. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth. Multi-core processing, static web serving, and database operations perform in the average tier. This hardware profile is suited for network-bound, multi-threaded workloads such as distributed database nodes, static web serving, and general-purpose applications requiring high network throughput without local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded and network-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for general-purpose and network-bound workloads.
tagline: A network-optimized general-purpose server featuring sixty-four virtual cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and seventy-five gigabits per second baseline network throughput.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong large-block read and write bandwidth
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network traffic
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Average performance in multi-core processing, database operations, and web serving
Suited for network-bound general-purpose applications and distributed databases
